verdict.summary: Round 3 finds no correctness, soundness, security, or test-validity issue. The proposal remains explicitly documentation-only and audit-gated, consistently describes the mechanism as issuer self-attestation rather than verifier-checkable conformance, and repeatedly preserves the honest-issuer assumption against malicious or compromised issuers. Historical credentials remain excluded through the epoch or signature-marker rule. Leaf collapse remains fail-closed and limited to an explicitly enumerated, audited per-datatype injectivity allow-list, while many-to-one datatypes retain a separate identity handle. The companion field-native design is updated consistently, and all ZK claims retain the required not-externally-audited and sq-qhy4 caveats. Relative to round 2, the findings remain materially unchanged at zero, so progress is stagnant under the required scale.
